canonical_pdb_caveat: 5E0V is NOT apo wild-type PCNA: it is the PCNA S228I disease variant in complex with a FEN1 peptide (Duffy CM et al. J Mol Biol. 2016;428:1023-1040, PMID 26688547). It is retained as the project's nominal reference ID only. Residue numbering below is canonical UniProt P12004 and was derived from wild-type complexes (1AXC, 8GLA, 5YD8), NOT from 5E0V.
generator: Advay parallel track 4; literature + structural derivation
numbering_note: All residue numbers are canonical UniProt P12004 numbering (1-261). For the human PCNA PDB entries used here (1AXC, 8GLA, 5YD8, 5E0V), PDB author numbering follows UniProt P12004; ordered residues span ~1-255 (C-terminal tail often disordered). No residue number in this file is invented: each region cites a PDB structure and/or a PMID, and structurally derived lists are reproducible via scripts/derive_pcna_interface_contacts.py.
contact_method: Structurally derived lists use heavy-atom (non-hydrogen) distance <= 4.5 A. Literature lists are quoted from the cited paper.
claim_guidance: Overlap of a prediction with any region below is 'overlaps a known PCNA interaction interface', NOT a novel site (governance doc 12). The AOH1996/8GLA region is a positive-control region; recovery is a sanity check and never validates a novel-site prediction.

pip_box_binding_site.description: Front-face hydrophobic pocket that binds canonical PIP-box (PIPM) motifs (e.g. p21, FEN1, DNA pol delta p66). Spans a hydrophobic pocket, the IDCL, and the C-terminal region.
pip_box_binding_site.source_citation: Mueller R et al. The PCNA interaction motifs revisited: thinking outside the PIP-box. Cell Mol Life Sci. 2019. PMID 31134302 (pocket residues 40-44, 117-135, 230-235, 251-253).

structural_support.citation: Gulbis JM, Kelman Z, Hurwitz J, O'Donnell M, Kuriyan J. Cell. 1996;87:297-306. PMID 8861913.
structural_support.method: PCNA residues within 4.5 A of the bound p21 PIP-box peptide (p21 res 143-160)
structural_support.note: The structural contact set is broader than the core pocket because the p21 peptide extends across the PCNA surface (Gulbis 1996). The 'residues' field uses the conservative literature core pocket.

apim_site.description: AlkB-homologue-2 PCNA-interacting motif (APIM) binding site. Structural work shows APIM binds the SAME front-face PIPM pocket as the PIP-box; APIM is now classified as a non-canonical PIPM subgroup.
apim_site.source_citation: Hara K et al. Structure of PCNA bound to an APIM peptide reveals the universality of PCNA interaction. Acta Crystallogr F. 2018;74:214-221. PMID 29633969. APIM contacts Met40, Leu47, Leu126, Gly127, Ile128, Pro129, Val233, Pro234.
apim_site.motif_discovery_citation: Gilljam KM et al. Identification of a novel, widespread, and functionally important PCNA-binding motif. J Cell Biol. 2009;186:645-654. PMID 19752023.
apim_site.note: Overlaps pip_box_binding_site; not an independent surface.

idcl.description: Interdomain connecting loop. Flexible loop linking the two PCNA domains; forms part of the PIPM binding pocket and is a key recognition element for PCNA partners.
idcl.source_citation: Mueller R et al. Cell Mol Life Sci. 2019. PMID 31134302 (IDCL residues 117-135). The Track-4 brief's '~119-133' is a subset of this range.

trimer_interface.description: Head-to-tail subunit-subunit interface of the PCNA homotrimer (the ring-closing contacts). Burial of these residues forms the closed clamp.
trimer_interface.source_citation: Derived from PDB 1AXC (Gulbis JM et al. Cell. 1996;87:297-306. PMID 8861913), inter-subunit heavy-atom contacts among trimer chains A/C/E within 4.5 A. Reproduce via scripts/derive_pcna_interface_contacts.py.
trimer_interface.note: Structurally derived; the underlying trimer architecture is established (Krishna TS et al. Cell. 1994;79:1233-1243, PMID 8001157, yeast PCNA 1PLQ; human trimer in 1AXC).

aoh1996_contact_region.description: PCNA residues contacting the AOH1996 derivative (AOH1996-1LE; ligand code ZQZ) in the co-crystal structure 8GLA. POSITIVE-CONTROL region.
aoh1996_contact_region.source_citation: Derived from PDB 8GLA (Gu L et al. Small molecule targeting of transcription-replication conflict for selective chemotherapy. Cell Chem Biol. 2023;30:1235. PMID 37531956), PCNA residues within 4.5 A of ligand ZQZ. Reproduce via scripts/derive_pcna_interface_contacts.py.
aoh1996_contact_region.method: heavy-atom distance <= 4.5 A to ligand ZQZ
aoh1996_contact_region.overlap_observation: This region substantially overlaps pip_box_binding_site / IDCL / C-terminus (shared residues include 40, 44-47, 121, 123-126, 128, 129, 131, 232-234, 250-253). The ZQZ ligand also contacts residues across two PCNA chains in 8GLA. Per governance doc 12, recovery of this region is positive-control overlap with known interfaces, NOT evidence of a novel druggable site.
